title: package.json rewrite + TS/ESLint(flat,^9)/Tailwind-v4/Next-16 config
notes: Foundation; must merge FIRST before W2. Hard gate: npm install && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run build clean. lint must run without the getFilename TypeError (ESLint pinned ^9, NOT ^10). eslint-config-next major must equal next major (16). Tailwind v4: @tailwindcss/postcss plugin, not v3 shape.

npm run lint passes with --max-warnings 0 and no getFilename TypeError

npm run build clean
scripts block matches the binding architect spec exactly
eslint.config.mjs uses array-spread flat-config shape

title: Domain + data + filtering (types.ts, books.ts [4/3/2 seed], filtering.ts [all-by-sum])
notes: Reviewed under the U2+U3+U4 integration Skeptic. books.ts seed is FIXED 4 to-read / 3 reading / 2 finished (load-bearing for the intro bug, not Worker discretion). filtering.ts: counts.all = SUM of three per-status buckets, NOT books.length (binding contract). filtering.ts carries a module manifest header. Single importer (BookList.tsx) so no 5-importer shared-utility trigger.

title: Components (BookCard, FilterTabs, BookList) + wire home page
notes: Closes the U2+U3+U4 integration Skeptic AND QA scenarios 1 + 5. BookList owns filter state as a client component; BookList.tsx carries a module manifest. Consumes countByStatus/filterBooks from U3, renders inside U2 shell. QA fires here (concurrent with the integration Skeptic): scenario 1 (home loads with grid + tabs) and scenario 5 (badges equal grid counts: To Read=4, Reading=3, Finished=2, All=9). This is the main-finalization gate boundary.

title: README reality-check - cross-check every reality-asserting README claim against shipped main + three tier branches
notes: Final integration gate; runs AFTER all three tier branches exist. Claim-by-claim: route list vs src/app/; 'three branches' vs actual branch set; install one-liner verbatim vs real bootstrap.sh URL; opt-in activation step vs AGENTS.md; per-tier how-to-start vs each branch TASK.md path+spec level; stated counts (9 books, three exercises/tiers); npm script names vs package.json (no 'next lint'); Node floor vs U1 engines (20.9+). Skeptic: doc-sync brief - Major if drift would mislead a reader, Minor for cosmetic count mismatch.
